ANALYSIS · ZA-DIVERGENT · OPEN SOURCE
✎ Analysis Crime & Security

MEGA Account Compromise: Credential Stuffing via 3xK Tech GmbH Proxy Infrastructure

Automated credential stuffing attack detected across three proxy nodes operated by 3xK Tech GmbH (AS200373). MegaApiClient session indicates active data exfiltration.

By ZA-DIVERGENT Staff 19 Jun 2026 - 14:35
credential-stuffing data-breach proxy-infrastructure mega cyber

Incident Summary

On 19 June 2026 at 15:53 SAST, an unauthorized login to a personal MEGA cloud storage account was detected. The session originated from IP address 209.50.171.190 using the user-agent string Go-http-client/1.1 — the default identifier for Go's standard HTTP library, indicating an automated tool rather than a human operating a browser.

Within 26 minutes, two additional sessions were opened from the same infrastructure:

| Time (SAST) | IP Address | Location | Client | Role | |---|---|---|---|---| | 15:53 | 209.50.171.190 | Ashburn, Virginia | Go-http-client/1.1 | Initial credential test | | 16:19 | 104.207.47.133 | Ashburn, Virginia | Unknown | Second session | | 16:19 | 209.50.190.92 | Berlin, Germany | MegaApiClient | API bulk download |

Infrastructure Attribution

All three IP addresses belong to the same operator: 3xK Tech GmbH, a German hosting provider operating under ASN AS200373.

  • Headquarters: Altenhofer Weg 21, Schorfheide, 16244, Germany
  • Abuse contact: friedrich.kraeft@3xktech.cloud / +4917641256819
  • Network classification: Hosting provider (all three IPs tagged as Proxy by IPinfo and Shodan)

Shodan reconnaissance of the primary IP (209.50.171.190) revealed:

  • Port 22 (SSH): Returning "Exceeded MaxStartups" — indicative of high-volume automated connections, consistent with botnet or credential stuffing infrastructure.
  • Port 3128 (HTTP Proxy): Running an authenticated HTTP proxy (407 Proxy Authentication Required) — confirming this is a proxy infrastructure node.
  • Port 8081 (HTTP): Serving a Let's Encrypt certificate valid for only 7 days (2026-06-12 to 2026-06-19) with a Subject Alternative Name pointing to a different IP address (74.119.149.39) — indicating a shared, rotating proxy cluster.
  • Port 179 (BGP): Open — unusual for a single server, suggesting routing-level infrastructure.

3xK Tech GmbH has extensive abuse history across multiple IPs in the AS200373 range, with reports on AbuseIPDB (46+ on individual addresses), FraudGuard threat intelligence, Scamalytics fraud database, and CleanTalk spam blacklists.

Attack Vector: Credential Stuffing

The attack pattern is consistent with credential stuffing — the automated testing of stolen username/password pairs against online services. MEGA has a documented history of such attacks, with 15,500+ users compromised in a known incident where passwords reused across breached services were tested against MEGA login endpoints.

The attack sequence observed here follows the standard credential stuffing playbook:

  1. Credential test (15:53): Automated Go HTTP client tests a stolen email/password pair against MEGA login from one proxy node.
  2. Session establishment (16:19): Upon successful authentication, two additional sessions are opened from different nodes in the same proxy cluster.
  3. Data exfiltration (16:19): The MegaApiClient session — using MEGA's official SDK/API client — begins programmatic bulk file access. This is not a browser session; it is automated data download.

The use of multiple IPs from different geographic locations (Virginia, Germany) within the same ASN is a deliberate operational security measure — distributing sessions across proxy nodes to avoid triggering single-IP rate limits.

Assessment

Confidence: HIGH. This is a coordinated, automated credential stuffing and data exfiltration operation conducted through 3xK Tech GmbH proxy infrastructure. The credentials were almost certainly obtained from a prior breach of a third-party service where the same password was reused.

Indicators of Compromise

| Indicator | Type | Context | |---|---|---| | 209.50.171.190 | IPv4 | Initial credential test node | | 104.207.47.133 | IPv4 | Secondary session node | | 209.50.190.92 | IPv4 | MegaApiClient exfiltration node | | 74.119.149.39 | IPv4 | SSL SAN cross-reference (shared cert cluster) | | AS200373 | ASN | 3xK Tech GmbH — all three IPs | | Go-http-client/1.1 | User-Agent | Automated credential testing tool | | MegaApiClient | Client ID | MEGA SDK — programmatic bulk download | | 209.50.171.0/24 | Network | Primary attack subnet | | 104.207.47.0/24 | Network | Secondary attack subnet | | 209.50.190.0/24 | Network | Exfiltration subnet |

Sources

  • IPinfo.io geolocation and classification data for all three IPs
  • Shodan host reconnaissance (209.50.171.190)
  • AbuseIPDB, FraudGuard, Scamalytics, CleanTalk blacklist records for AS200373
  • BGPView ASN record for AS200373
  • SecurityBrief NZ — MEGA credential stuffing incident (15,000+ users)
  • OWASP Credential Stuffing documentation
